Meaning.En: to hold someone responsible for a fault or wrong; to express criticism or disapproval of someone for a mistake or bad outcome
Meaning.Example: She blamed her colleague for the missed deadline.

Situation.Vi: Sau khi dự án thất bại, sếp đã đổ lỗi cho nhóm kỹ thuật.
Situation.En: After the project failed, the manager blamed the engineering team.

Situation.Vi: Trẻ em thường đổ lỗi cho anh/chị/em khi làm vỡ đồ.
Situation.En: Children often blame their siblings when they break something.

Meaning.Vi: sự đổ lỗi; trách nhiệm (được gán cho người khác)
Meaning.En: responsibility for a fault or wrong; something for which someone is held responsible
Meaning.Example: The blame for the accident fell on the driver’s inattention.

Situation.Vi: Sau tai nạn, cả hai bên đều từ chối nhận trách nhiệm.
Situation.En: After the accident, both parties refused to accept blame.

Situation.Vi: Chính phủ bị đặt vào vị thế phải chịu trách nhiệm về khủng hoảng kinh tế.
Situation.En: The government was placed in a position where it had to shoulder the blame for the economic crisis.
